news.t: These two subsectors of technology are trading at a discount to S&P 500
Two key ETFs to watch as technology stocks lose momentum

holdings.infoBox: XSW is an equity ETF with a total of 137 individual holdings. The top holdings are Arteris stock at 1.90%, Digital Turbine at 1.77%, Hut 8 at 1.60%, TeraWulf at 1.26%, and Riot Platforms at 1.26%.

overview.description: The State Street SPDR S&P Software & Services ETF (XSW) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in information technology equity. The fund tracks an equal-weighted index of software & services companies, as defined by GICS. XSW was launched on Sep 28, 2011 and is issued by State Street.

data.title: Software companies' business models are 'under assault' from AI
data.text: Software stocks have been pummeled on worries that AI will start eating into their business. EMJ Capital founder and president Eric Jackson says he is "very bullish" on AI, but that the sell-off in le...

data.title: High valuations in software are justified with use of AI agents, says BNP's Stefan Slowinski
data.text: Stefan Slowinski, BNP Paribas Exane global head of software research, joins CNBC's 'The Exchange' to discuss outlooks on software, the emergence of AI agents, and more.

data.title: These two subsectors of technology are trading at a discount to S&P 500
data.text: The ongoing sell-off in technology has created ample opportunities for investors, particularly in software and semiconductors, both of which are trading at a discount to the S&P 500. Paul Hickey dubs ...

data.title: Kindred Venture's Maqubela likes software companies
data.text: Kanyi Maqubela, Kindred Ventures founding partner, joins 'TechCheck' to discuss how he is considering headwinds for technology stocks, where he sees growth in enterprise software companies and more.

data.title: Two key ETFs to watch as technology stocks lose momentum
data.text: Software and online retail are particularly resilient when it comes to weakness in the broader technology space, exchange-traded fund analysts say.

data.title: Buying slowly resumes after tech's sell-off—How these ETFs are faring
data.text: CNBC's Bob Pisani talks about software and cloud-computing stocks and how trading activity is impacting ETFs with Tom Lydon, CEO of ETF Trends, and Andrew McOrmond of WallachBeth Capital.
